reasoning: Historically, Bitcoin closes the year higher than its starting price roughly 78% of the time (11 out of 14 years). Furthermore, 2025 is the year following the 2024 halving; in previous post-halving years (2013, 2017, 2021) Bitcoin consistently closed higher than it opened. Although the starting baseline of $93,429.20 is historically high, the macroeconomic tailwinds, including Donald Trump's stated pro-crypto policies and executive actions, provide strong potential upside. Considering real-world drift relative to its volatility, the probability of closing above its starting price is estimated at around 67%.
